# Axocoatl — Rust-Native Agentic AI Runtime
> Coordination-first, actor-based, always-on agents in Rust

Axocoatl is an open-source agentic runtime for building self-coordinating
multi-agent systems. Top-level agents activate via stigmergic pheromone signals —
no central orchestrator. Built on ractor actors with per-agent token budgets,
four-tier memory, and checkpointing for crash recovery. Local-first: your
hardware, your model, your data, zero telemetry.

## Quick Start

### YAML Configuration (recommended)
```yaml
agents:
  - id: researcher
    provider: ollama
    model: llama3.2
    system_prompt: "You are a research assistant. Provide detailed factual answers."
    depends_on: []

  - id: summarizer
    provider: ollama
    model: llama3.2
    system_prompt: "Summarize the input in 1-2 sentences."
    depends_on: [researcher]

workflows:
  - id: research-and-summarize
    name: "Research and Summarize"
    agents: [researcher, summarizer]
    entry_point: researcher

providers:
  ollama:
    base_url: "http://localhost:11434"
```

### CLI
```bash
axocoatl onboard                   # Interactive setup wizard
axocoatl doctor                    # Environment / dependency health check
axocoatl init my-project           # Scaffold a project non-interactively
axocoatl validate                  # Check config
axocoatl dev                       # Development mode (IPC + HTTP)
axocoatl serve                     # Production HTTP server
axocoatl chat --agent assistant    # Interactive chat
axocoatl workflow list             # List configured workflows
axocoatl workflow run <id> -i "…"  # Execute a workflow
```

## Core Concepts

### Agents
Persistent ractor actors that process tasks. Each agent has: a provider (LLM), tools
(built-in and MCP-discovered), four-tier memory, and a token budget. A supervision
loop restarts a crashed agent from its last checkpoint.

### Providers
Ollama, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral — six providers,
provider-agnostic, no vendor lock-in. The OpenAI provider targets any
OpenAI-compatible endpoint via `base_url` (LM Studio, MLX, vLLM), sending each
agent's configured model per request.

### Workflows
Multi-agent pipelines defined in YAML. Agents declare `depends_on` to form a DAG.
Execution is driven by the EventLattice — no polling, no central scheduler.

### Coordination
Stigmergic EventLattice: top-level agents self-activate via pheromone signals with
exponential decay. When an agent completes, it publishes a TaskCompleted event;
downstream agents accumulate signal until their threshold is crossed, then activate
with upstream outputs as context. No central orchestrator.

For work that needs runtime decomposition, an agent with `role: coordinator` breaks a
goal into subtasks and assigns each to a spawned worker via a capability + budget
auction, runs them in parallel, and synthesizes the results. Decomposition uses a
symbolic HTN planner when an `htn_methods_file` is configured, otherwise the LLM. The
coordinator is itself one lattice node, so the "no orchestrator" property holds
between top-level agents. The pass is resumable via checkpointing.

### Token budgets
Per-agent pre-flight token budgets. When a call would exceed the budget the policy is
`abort` (default) or `warn`. Under context-window pressure the session is compacted
automatically (summarized toward the model window); nothing is lost — the raw
conversation stays in the daily log and semantic memory.

### Protocols
- MCP: connect to external stdio and streamable-HTTP MCP servers, discover their
  tools, and call them over a persistent client. Axocoatl is also an MCP server
  (`axocoatl mcp serve` exposes each agent as a tool).
- A2A: inbound A2A server (`/.well-known/agent.json` + `/a2a/tasks`) that accepts
  tasks from other agents. No outbound A2A client.
- Webhooks: opt-in outbound event egress — the lattice POSTs signed events to
  configured endpoints (the outbound counterpart to inbound A2A). A default install
  makes no outbound webhook requests.

### Memory
Four tiers plus checkpointing:
- Session — the live conversation.
- Daily log — append-only JSONL by date; lossless raw archive, readable by date range.
- Core memory — named, agent-editable blocks (persona / human / project) rendered into
  the prompt each turn; shareable across agents.
- Semantic — on-device vector recall (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via Candle); passive top-k
  injection plus an agent-driven recall tool.

A background "sleep-time" pass consolidates idle agents' memory (promotes durable facts
into core blocks). Checkpointing snapshots the session to disk for crash recovery.

### Isolation
Each directory session runs its file/shell/terminal tools inside a rootless, hardened
Podman container: only the session directory is bind-mounted, dangerous capabilities
are dropped, `no-new-privileges` is set, and the network can be isolated. It bounds a
misbehaving agent's blast radius; it is not a defense against prompt injection acting
within the workspace.

## HTTP API

```
GET  /health                              # Health check
GET  /api/agents                          # List agents
POST /api/agents/{id}/execute             # Execute single agent
GET  /api/agents/{id}/status              # Agent status
GET  /api/workflows                       # List workflows
POST /api/workflows/{id}/execute          # Execute workflow
GET  /ws                                  # WebSocket streaming
```

## API Reference
See: https://github.com/axocoatl/axocoatl
